description.en: A soft, pliable wheat flatbread enriched with lard, rolled thin and griddled just a minute a side until lightly spotted. The everyday wrap of the US-Mexico borderlands, holding burritos, breakfast tacos and quesadillas alike.
description.de: Ein weiches, biegsames Weizen-Fladenbrot mit Schweineschmalz, dünn ausgerollt und nur eine Minute pro Seite auf der Platte gebacken, bis es leicht gefleckt ist. Die alltägliche Hülle der US-mexikanischen Grenzregion, für Burritos, Frühstückstacos und Quesadillas.
description.es: Un pan plano de trigo, suave y flexible, enriquecido con manteca, estirado fino y cocido apenas un minuto por lado en el comal hasta dorarse ligeramente. El envoltorio cotidiano de la frontera entre México y Estados Unidos, para burritos, tacos de desayuno y quesadillas.
description.fr: Un pain plat de blé, souple et tendre, enrichi au saindoux, étalé fin et cuit à peine une minute de chaque côté sur le comal jusqu'à légèrement se tacheter. L'enveloppe quotidienne de la frontière américano-mexicaine, pour burritos, tacos du matin et quesadillas.

origin.note: Sonoran in origin; became the everyday bread of the whole US Southwest through border and migrant cooking.

keeping.note: Keeps soft several days sealed at room temperature thanks to the fat content; reheat briefly on a dry skillet. Freezes well stacked with parchment.

method.title: Mix dry
method.summary: Whisk flour, baking powder and salt, then rub or cut in the lard until crumbly.

method.title: Add water
method.summary: Pour in hot water and mix to a shaggy dough; the heat helps develop just enough gluten.

method.title: Knead
method.summary: Knead 3-5 minutes until smooth, soft and elastic; rest briefly only if it resists rolling.

method.title: Divide
method.summary: Portion into small balls and let them relax a few minutes.

method.title: Roll thin
method.summary: Roll each ball into a thin, even round.

method.title: Griddle
method.summary: Cook on a hot dry comal about a minute per side until lightly spotted, pressing down any air pockets with a folded towel.

method.title: Keep warm
method.summary: Stack in a cloth or tortilla warmer to stay soft and pliable.

source.adaptation: Hydration recalculated from the source's cup measurements using standard gram conversions (125 g/cup flour, 237 g/cup water); notably higher than the source's own self-reported figure.
